projects.name: FutureScaping Coastal Change Demo
projects.description: A client-safe demonstration of repeat survey reporting for one monitored reach, combining aerial imagery, sections, panorama context, weather history, and change workflow preview in one interface.

site.name: Monitored Estuary Demo Site
site.context: This demo shows how one monitored reach can be reviewed across repeat survey rounds without revealing the live project location.

programme.operationalLesson: A single monitored reach is enough to demonstrate how the reporting workflow supports interpretation, comparison, and client discussion.
programme.consolidationGoal: Keep the interface stable while the survey rounds and supporting outputs update over time.

surveyAssetModel.note: This demo keeps one monitored reach only, using the same repeat-survey structure as the full monitoring system.

surveys.label: Survey Round 1 - 22 and 23 March 2026
surveys.notes: Baseline round used as the starting point for later comparison.

surveys.label: Survey Round 2 - 18 and 19 April 2026
surveys.notes: First repeat round for visual and section comparison.

surveys.label: Survey Round 3 - 16 and 17 June 2026
surveys.notes: Latest repeat round for longer-range comparison.

area8.tideWindow: Falling tide well ahead of low water
area8.missionRole: Opened the repeat survey day and captured the monitored upper reach before low tide.
area8.operationalNote: This block used the first two flights of the day, creating a long early-tide comparison window before the lowest water level was reached.
area8.weatherNotes: Day 1, 18 April 2026. Early survey window with the tide still falling and a broad extent of standing water remaining in the monitored reach.
area8.surveyNotes: This was the first block covered on the April repeat day. It provides an early-tide snapshot that can be compared against the later, more exposed rounds.
area8.purpose: Shows the monitored reach well before low water, helping explain how exposed-ground visibility changes through the survey window.

area8.tideWindow: Later flood tide well after low water
area8.missionRole: Closed the June repeat survey with a final capture of the monitored reach after the tide had turned.
area8.operationalNote: The team paused briefly for light rain before launching, then completed the block during a calm late-session weather window.
area8.weatherNotes: Day 2, 17 June 2026. Light rain briefly threatened the capture, but conditions stayed workable and never developed into a heavier shower.
area8.surveyNotes: This later-tide June block shows the same monitored reach under a very different water-state, making it useful for explaining why survey timing matters as much as the imagery itself.

2026-04-18.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside the fixed Area 3 change footprint.
2026-04-18.cellSize: Survey grid size 0.10 m with a reporting threshold of +/-0.05 m.

area3.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside the fixed Area 3 change footprint.
area3.cellSize: Survey grid size 0.10 m with a reporting threshold of +/-0.05 m.
area3.notes: Area 3 combines the hosted 3D viewer, downloaded classification imagery, and a plain-English measured summary for the live change-analysis page.
area3.previewBaselineCaption: Plan-view height change surface for Area 3, showing where the later survey sits higher or lower across the measured footprint.
area3.previewCurrentCaption: Simplified gain-and-loss classification for the same Area 3 comparison, useful as a quick client-facing reference image.
area3.viewerSummary: Interactive terrain preview for Area 3 between 22-23 March 2026 and 18-19 April 2026. Use it with the measured totals and reference maps to inspect where sediment appears to have built up or lowered.

polygons.label: Area 3 change footprint
polygons.summary: Net accretion across the current Area 3 test footprint, with meaningful local loss still visible in parts of the compared surface.
polygons.notes: Aggregate Area 3 summary for the first hosted change-analysis page.

area8.method: Visual comparison workflow preview for the monitored reach.
area8.notes: This demo area is ready for quantified change reporting, but the current client-safe version focuses on the workflow and visual comparison rather than published cubic-metre totals.

2026-06-16.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside the fixed Area 3 change footprint.
2026-06-16.cellSize: Survey grid size 0.10 m with a reporting threshold of +/-0.05 m.

area3.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside the fixed Area 3 change footprint.
area3.cellSize: Survey grid size 0.10 m with a reporting threshold of +/-0.05 m.
area3.notes: Area 3 now frames the story from the first March survey through to the latest June survey, with the intermediate April round feeding the wider three-round analysis underneath.
area3.previewBaselineCaption: Plan-view height change surface for Area 3, used here as part of the wider March-to-June story so people can see where the latest survey sits higher or lower within the measured footprint.
area3.previewCurrentCaption: Simplified gain-and-loss classification for the same Area 3 review, useful as a quick client-facing reference image inside the wider March-to-June story.
area3.viewerSummary: Interactive terrain preview for Area 3 between 22-23 March 2026 and 16-17 June 2026. Use it with the measured totals and reference maps to inspect where sediment appears to have built up or lowered across the wider monitoring window.

polygons.label: Area 3 change footprint
polygons.summary: Net lowering across the current Area 3 test footprint between April and June, with the later survey ending up lower overall.
polygons.notes: Aggregate Area 3 summary for the April-to-June change-analysis page.

area8.method: Visual comparison workflow preview for the monitored reach.
area8.notes: This latest survey pair is included to show how the same reach can be revisited over time even before the measured volume outputs are added to the client-safe demo.

workflow.title: Capture the same reach repeatedly
workflow.copy: Survey the same monitored reach on repeat dates so the imagery and profile lines stay comparable through time.

workflow.title: Review visual change first
workflow.copy: Use aerial imagery, panorama context, and sections to understand where the shape and exposure of the reach appears to be changing.

workflow.title: Add measured reporting
workflow.copy: Once the change workflow is processed, the same interface can show quantified build-up, lowering, and net balance.

workflow.title: Scale to more areas later
workflow.copy: The same structure can expand from this single-reach demo to a larger multi-area monitoring programme.
